methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Isomorphic Labs is a private-stage biotech company founded by Demis Hassabis that has raised $600M to date. The valuation universe comprises 8 private peers in Biotech & Health alongside 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L), though the peer set lacks sufficient clustering by stage or valuation tier for robust median-based analysis.

Peer-median triangulation is not applicable; while 8 same-sector private peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier companies yielded fewer than 3 datapoints in each category, falling below the threshold for statistical reliability. Public-comp anchoring using the Biotech & Health sector P/S median of 6.9x is also not applicable, as Isomorphic Labs does not publicly disclose revenue—a limitation common to private companies. The identified public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) provide context on sector multiples but cannot directly value the target without disclosed financials.

The absence of both sufficiently granular peer clustering and revenue disclosure prevents derivation of a quantitative valuation range through standard triangulation methods. Further analysis would require either: (1) disclosure or estimation of Isomorphic Labs' revenue and profitability metrics, (2) closer identification of stage-matched private comparables with disclosed valuations, or (3) expansion of the public comp set. The $600M raised to date establishes a floor for investor commitment but does not independently support a fair-value estimate without additional operating or market data.
